item: WE-3 - wire: appearance-hash on unit records (AUX)
note: AUX stays plain JSON per the spec intro ('Wire host: W-A AUX channel ... this spec is that renegotiation, kept JSON') -- confirmed against world_stream.cpp (kTypeAux frame body is UTF-8 JSON, deflated only over the 8KB threshold, decoded client-side with JSON.parse in web/js/dwf-ws.js:468) and against tile_map_dump.cpp's shared emit_units(). No BLOCK_SET-style binary record grammar applies to AUX, so no change was needed to wire_v1.h/.cpp or tools/harness/gen_wire_fixture.mjs / wire_decode_test.mjs -- those cover BLOCK_SET tile records only.

legacy_json_path_GET_mapdata_excerpt.endpoint: GET /mapdata?player=probe&x=0&y=0&z=100&w=80&h=50 (per-player camera_for_player default = host window, actually resolved to origin 70,85,160)
legacy_json_path_GET_mapdata_excerpt.observation: miner-class unit (id 5505) carries ah/sw/sh/ax/ay; sw=2 matches the spec fixture ('miner-class unit wielding tool reports sw=2'). Units without a live composite (e.g. cats, or dwarves emit_units skips before export) simply omit ah/sw/sh/ax/ay -- byte-identical to pre-WE-3 shape otherwise.

5725.note: id resolved via dfhack lua: creature_id=CAT, texpos=[0,0,0,0,0,0] -- DF never composited it (flat animal), so no ah, exactly per spec ('flat animals carry none')

v1_aux_path_ws_probe_auxdump.aux_frame_rate: 453 AUX frames / 15.0s = 30.1/s, 0 gaps>500ms, all v1 acks accounted for
v1_aux_path_ws_probe_auxdump.hash_format_check: bad_hash_count=0 -- every emitted ah matched ^[0-9a-f]{16}$ (ws_probe.py's new AUXDUMP mode regex-checks every ah value live)

unit_sprite_route_resolve_check.observation: 3658 (1x1 dwarf, matches spec fixture 'a 1x1 dwarf sw=sh=1'); 5505/5641 (miners, sw=2, matches 'miner-class unit reports sw=2'). Elephant-class (id 5522 and 6 others) present in the fort but had texpos all-zero (never composited -- DF only composites units it actually renders on some player's viewport; none of this fort's players' cameras have passed over an elephant yet). Optional per spec ('elephant-class if in view') -- not forced; would require driving the live host camera over one, which is WE-7 (composite warmer) territory, out of WE-3 scope.

perf_gate.note: run with capture-unit-census on + capture-unit-sprites on (both feature flags live end-to-end) and 0 foreign WS players attached (/diag checked before and after).

regression_check.method: agent-browser: open, wait 8s for streaming, console/errors dump, screenshot, close --all
regression_check.result: page loaded (title 'Dwf - Tiles'), /diag showed 1 connected legacy player streaming at ~30/s (delta mode), zero console messages, zero errors captured. Unknown JSON fields (ah/sw/sh/ax/ay) are silently ignored by the legacy renderer as expected -- no code there reads them.

code_changes: src/tile_map_dump.cpp: emit_units() -- one unit_sprite_snapshot() copy per call (skipped entirely when capture-unit-sprites is off), O(1) per-unit lookup; appends ah/sw/sh/ax/ay ONLY when a record with a non-empty hash exists. Shared verbatim by build_map_json_impl (full frame) and build_map_delta_for_camera (delta) -- both call sites unchanged.

src/world_stream.cpp: UnitRec gains ah/sw/sh/ax/ay (default empty/0); populated during the WA-9 single global units scan (one sprite_snapshot copy before the loop, per-unit O(1) lookup, same pattern); the per-connection AUX JSON assembly emits the fields only when ah is non-empty.

tools/harness/ws_probe.py: new 'auxdump' token -- decodes every v1 AUX frame (inflating deflated payloads), tracks each unit id's first-seen / first-ah timestamps, validates ah is 16 lowercase hex chars, and writes results/auxdump_<player>.json. Used as this item's live wire-contract verification instrument (no BLOCK_SET-style fixture change was in scope since AUX stays JSON).
